title: Editing Instead of Generating from Scratch
concept: Image editing starts from an existing real photo rather than pure noise, modifying it according to a new instruction while preserving its overall structure.

exercises.prompt: If you wanted to change just the sky in a photo from blue to sunset orange, what's the smartest starting point?

options.text: Start with the original photo and change only the sky
Start with random static noise and hope a similar photo appears
Delete the photo and describe it from memory

exercises.explain: Starting from the real photo keeps everything else (the ground, people, buildings) exactly the same while only the sky changes.

cards.title: Why Redraw the Whole Picture?
cards.body: Imagine you have a perfect photo of your dog, but you want it wearing a hat. Would you throw away the photo and try to draw a brand new dog from nothing? No way! You'd just add a hat to the photo you already have. That's exactly how AI image editing works.

cards.title: What Is Image Editing?
cards.body: Image editing means an AI starts with a real, existing photo (not random noise) and changes only what an instruction asks for — like 'add sunglasses' or 'make it night' — while keeping the rest of the picture's structure, layout, and details the same.

cards.title: Like Editing, Not Repainting
cards.body: Think of a house painter. If you ask them to repaint just your front door, they don't tear down the whole house and rebuild it. They keep the walls, windows, and roof exactly as they are, and only touch the door. Image editing AI works the same way — it keeps the 'house' and only repaints the requested part.

cards.title: Turning Day into Night
cards.body: You give the AI a real daytime photo of a city street and say 'make it look like nighttime.' The AI keeps every building, car, and person in the exact same place, but changes the lighting, sky color, and adds glowing windows and streetlights — the structure stays, only the mood changes.

cards.title: Myth: Editing Redraws Everything
cards.body: Myth: When AI edits a photo, it secretly creates a whole new image from scratch that just looks similar. Truth: Real image editing starts from the actual pixels of your original photo and preserves its structure — it doesn't throw it away and start over with noise like pure image generation does.

cards.title: Generating vs. Editing
cards.body: These two AI tasks start from very different places.

left.text: Starts from random noise and builds a brand-new image based only on a text description.

right.text: Starts from a real existing photo and changes just the part the instruction mentions.

exercises.prompt: What does an AI image editor start with?
exercises.explain: Editing begins with a real photo so the rest of the image stays the same while only the requested change is applied.

exercises.prompt: Image editing preserves the overall structure of the original photo while making the requested change.
exercises.explain: True! The whole point of editing is to keep the layout and structure while only modifying what the instruction asks for.

exercises.sentence: Unlike generating a new image from noise, editing starts from a real ___ and changes only part of it.
exercises.explain: Editing always begins with an actual existing photo, not random noise or unrelated data.

exercises.prompt: Which task is an example of 'editing instead of generating from scratch'?

options.text: Adding a birthday hat to an existing photo of a cat
Creating a totally new fantasy castle image from a text prompt with no starting photo
Writing a poem about the ocean

exercises.explain: Adding a hat to an existing cat photo changes just one part while keeping the rest of the real photo intact — that's editing.

exercises.prompt: A student uploads a real photo of their backyard and asks an AI to 'add snow on the ground.' What should happen if the AI is truly editing (not generating from scratch)?

options.text: The backyard's layout, trees, and fence stay the same, only snow is added to the ground
The AI ignores the photo and creates a completely new random winter scene
The AI deletes the photo and asks the student to describe their backyard in words
The AI turns the photo into a drawing of an unrelated snowy mountain

exercises.explain: True editing keeps the original photo's structure — the same backyard, trees, and fence — and only changes what was asked: adding snow.
